output.market_context: Expansion regime with stable rates (~3.63% fed funds), moderate GDP growth (1.6%), and 10Y at 4.49% — supportive of cyclicals/tech but consumer sentiment is weakening (49.8). Tape shows clear rotation: small caps, industrials, and chip names breaking out while mega-cap tech (MSFT, AMZN, META, GOOGL) is in correction.
themes: Semiconductor/AI capex leadership (MU, AMD, SMCI, AVGO)
Rotation out of Mag-7 mega caps into small caps and industrials
Insider selling cluster in mega-cap tech (META, GOOGL, AMD, AVGO, WMT)
Goldilocks macro narrative supports cyclicals (XLI, IWM, XLF)

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Powerful technical breakout with bullish news cycle ahead of Wednesday earnings; HBM/AI memory thesis intact.
ranked_candidates.narrative: MU shows the strongest technical setup in the universe (RSI 69, +64% 1M, 20D breakout, full bull stack) backed by high-buzz positive news around AI memory and the Anthropic deal. Earnings Wednesday is a binary catalyst — chase risk into print is real. No insider data, but momentum + news alignment is durable.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Top news polarity in universe on explosive AI server growth forecast; prior beat and re-acceleration.
ranked_candidates.narrative: Best news score (96.7, polarity 0.99) on AI server demand and Nvidia Vera Rubin tie-in, with +19.6% 1W momentum confirming. Technical is only mixed (price still below MA20) and sentiment buzz is muted, so this is news+momentum driven, not yet cross-signal. Watch for fade after one-day pop.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Industrials breakout aligned with expansion-regime favored sector and 'Goldilocks' narrative.
ranked_candidates.narrative: Clean 20D breakout, bull stack, RSI 64.6 with strong positive news flow on rotation away from AI. Macro regime explicitly favors Industrials. ETF so no insider/earnings, but every available signal aligns positively.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Small-cap breakout confirms broadening rally narrative in a stable-rate expansion regime.
ranked_candidates.narrative: 20D breakout, bull stack, RSI 63.7 with strong positive news on US 'Goldilocks' economy. Small caps benefit from stable Fed and broadening leadership. ETF so signals are macro/tech only, but they align cleanly.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Strong technicals and AI chip narrative offset by sizable CTO insider sale.
ranked_candidates.narrative: AMD has powerful momentum (+21.9% 1M, bull stack, RSI 62.4) and positive news/earnings beat, but the CTO sold $3.2M of stock — a single-insider sale (not a cluster) at the highs is a yellow flag, not a kill signal. Strong cross-signal alignment otherwise; size positions modestly.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Tech sector ETF with bull stack and macro regime favoring Technology.
ranked_candidates.narrative: RSI 60, bull stack, +8.4% 1M, positive news on tech advance. Macro regime explicitly favors Technology. Lacks individual-name catalysts but provides diversified exposure to the theme.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Broad tech beta with healthy technicals though mega-cap drag is a real headwind.
ranked_candidates.narrative: Bull stack and RSI 57.9 with positive news, but the underlying Mag-7 weakness (MSFT, AMZN, META, GOOGL all in correction) caps upside. Cleaner ways to express this view via XLK or XLI.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Financials bull stack with macro regime favorable; modest positive news polarity.
ranked_candidates.narrative: RSI 62.8, bull stack, +4% 1M. Macro regime favors Financials. News polarity modest (+0.15) but flow positive. Steady core position rather than alpha trade.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Blue-chip index ETF with bull stack and positive news flow amid mega-cap rotation.

output.summary: 5 weeks in, book is -1.4% vs SPY -0.7% (cum alpha -0.67%) with a 20% weekly win rate. The system's one real win — sizing MU/SMCI/NVDA into the late-May semis breakout (+25% basket) — has been largely given back by chasing breakouts at extension (MSFT -11.8%, AVGO -14.5%, SMCI -12.9%, AAPL -6.0%) where high technical+news scores masked weakening internals.
what_worked: Sizing the cleanest cross-signal name aggressively paid off once: MU at 5/25 (tech 82 + news 94, high conviction) returned +45.7% — the single best pick and validates the 'upsize the winner' lesson when factors align early in a move.

Tax-loss harvesting discipline executed cleanly: SMCI, AVGO, MSFT, AAPL, XLE, GOOGL, AMZN, BRK.B all cut on broken theses captured ~$99 realized gains offset and meaningful tax credits; no dead capital lingering.
High-conviction picks outperformed medium (33% win rate, +3.75% avg vs 17%/-1.88%) — conviction labeling has signal, even though absolute hit rate is low.
Avoiding insider-flag names (WMT, META, TSLA) was correct every week — those names continued to underperform; the hard-rule filter is earning its keep.
what_missed: Chasing 'technical + news' breakouts late in the move was the dominant failure mode: MSFT (-11.8% in 1 wk), AVGO (-14.5%), SMCI add (-12.9%), AAPL (-6.0%) — all entered with tech scores 69-91 and news 87-96 but immediately reversed. The composite score doesn't penalize extension enough.

6/01 was peak chase: opened MSFT (9%) and AVGO (6%) into RSI 75-82 universe-wide overbought conditions; both lost double-digits within a week. The reflection lesson to add into strength was applied without checking for exhaustion.

Cold-start 5/18 basket (XLE, BRK.B, GOOGL, AMZN) lost 3-5% each within 7 days — 'diversified medium-conviction' bucket has zero edge; all four closed at losses. Spreading thin without conviction destroys capital.
UNH ADD on 6/08 at RSI 70 / macro-mismatch right before a -5% week — high conviction overrode the explicit macro flag. Macro mismatch should be a hard size-cap, not a tiebreaker.
lessons_for_pm: Penalize RSI >70 and breakout-extension before sizing high. Last 4 high-tech-score opens at RSI 70+ (MSFT, AVGO, SMCI-add, AAPL) averaged -11% within 7 days. If RSI>70, cap conviction at medium and size at half.

News polarity 90+ has been a contrarian signal at extension: AVGO news 92, SMCI news 96, AAPL news 95 all preceded losses. Treat extreme news scores as crowded, not confirming, when the chart is already extended.
Macro-mismatch flag overrides news/technical — UNH 6/08 add lost on the macro tilt as flagged. Make macro_mismatch a hard 'no-add' rule, not a haircut.

Stop opening medium-conviction 6-8% positions to 'diversify' — the 5/18 basket and 6/15 ETF basket (DIA/XLF/XLI, all flat-to-down) show diversification without conviction = drag. Concentrate or hold cash.
Insider-flag avoidance is working — keep the hard rule at $30M. Don't soften it for 'cross-signal' names (AMD Lisa Su $57M was correctly held flat).

When a winner is sold/trimmed mid-trend (MU trim on 6/08 after CEO sale), document the give-back: MU continued to work after the trim. Insider sales at extension are right to respect, but consider trimming 25% not 50% when other signals remain green.
watch_for: Any new OPEN at RSI >70 with news polarity >90 — this is the documented loss pattern; require an explicit override rationale.
Concentration in remaining tech sleeve (NVDA + MU + AMD + new XLF/XLI/DIA ETFs) — if semis roll over, the ETF diversifiers are too small to cushion. Check correlation, not just sector labels.

Cash drag — after 8 closes in 2 weeks, dry powder is high but reflexively redeploying into 'cleanest cross-signal' names has been the loss pattern. Prefer waiting for pullbacks over chasing the next breakout.

output.summary: 5 weeks in, book is -1.4% vs SPY -0.7% (cum alpha -0.67%) with a 20% weekly win rate. The system's one real win — sizing MU/SMCI/NVDA into the late-May semis breakout (+25% basket) — has been largely given back by chasing breakouts at extension (MSFT -11.8%, AVGO -14.5%, SMCI -12.9%, AAPL -6.0%) where high technical+news scores masked weakening internals.
what_worked: Sizing the cleanest cross-signal name aggressively paid off once: MU at 5/25 (tech 82 + news 94, high conviction) returned +45.7% — the single best pick and validates the 'upsize the winner' lesson when factors align early in a move.

Tax-loss harvesting discipline executed cleanly: SMCI, AVGO, MSFT, AAPL, XLE, GOOGL, AMZN, BRK.B all cut on broken theses captured ~$99 realized gains offset and meaningful tax credits; no dead capital lingering.
High-conviction picks outperformed medium (33% win rate, +3.75% avg vs 17%/-1.88%) — conviction labeling has signal, even though absolute hit rate is low.
Avoiding insider-flag names (WMT, META, TSLA) was correct every week — those names continued to underperform; the hard-rule filter is earning its keep.
what_missed: Chasing 'technical + news' breakouts late in the move was the dominant failure mode: MSFT (-11.8% in 1 wk), AVGO (-14.5%), SMCI add (-12.9%), AAPL (-6.0%) — all entered with tech scores 69-91 and news 87-96 but immediately reversed. The composite score doesn't penalize extension enough.

6/01 was peak chase: opened MSFT (9%) and AVGO (6%) into RSI 75-82 universe-wide overbought conditions; both lost double-digits within a week. The reflection lesson to add into strength was applied without checking for exhaustion.

Cold-start 5/18 basket (XLE, BRK.B, GOOGL, AMZN) lost 3-5% each within 7 days — 'diversified medium-conviction' bucket has zero edge; all four closed at losses. Spreading thin without conviction destroys capital.

Macro-mismatch flag overrides news/technical — UNH 6/08 add lost on the macro tilt as flagged. Make macro_mismatch a hard 'no-add' rule, not a haircut.

Stop opening medium-conviction 6-8% positions to 'diversify' — the 5/18 basket and 6/15 ETF basket (DIA/XLF/XLI, all flat-to-down) show diversification without conviction = drag. Concentrate or hold cash.
Insider-flag avoidance is working — keep the hard rule at $30M. Don't soften it for 'cross-signal' names (AMD Lisa Su $57M was correctly held flat).

When a winner is sold/trimmed mid-trend (MU trim on 6/08 after CEO sale), document the give-back: MU continued to work after the trim. Insider sales at extension are right to respect, but consider trimming 25% not 50% when other signals remain green.

output.weekly_thesis: Lean into the rotation — add cyclicals (XLI, IWM) where signals align without extension; trim macro-mismatched UNH; respect the reflection lessons by NOT chasing MU into RSI 69 + news 92 print.
output.narrative: The tape is rotating cleanly: small caps and industrials breaking out while Mag-7 corrects and AI memory extends. I'm adding to XLI (+5%) and opening IWM (7%) — both clean breakouts in macro-favored areas with no extension risk, addressing the reflection note that pure ETF diversification without conviction is drag. I'm explicitly NOT adding MU despite the #1 score because RSI 69 + news 92 is the exact documented loss pattern, and chasing into Wednesday earnings is the wrong R/R. UNH gets a 50% trim — macro_mismatch is now a hard rule per reflection, and the Berkshire/Tepper healthcare-dumping news degrades the signal further. NVDA TLH skipped: $19 tax savings doesn't justify ceding AI exposure when news/earnings are still positive and the chart is basing.

overrides.reason: RSI 69 + news polarity 92 matches documented loss pattern (AVGO/SMCI/AAPL averaged -11% in 7 days from this setup). Binary earnings Wednesday adds chase risk. Hold the core, refuse the chase.
Tax savings too small to justify exiting AI exposure into a basing chart with positive news/earnings. Will revisit if loss deepens.
Reflection lesson #3 elevates macro_mismatch to hard rule. Combined with degraded_signal flag (healthcare-dumping news), the technical alone doesn't justify the position.
